ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-14-298386 Conservatorship of John Cox and
PTR-14-298385 The John C. cox Trust

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa appeared by zoom for Bennett Hong (conservator/trustee).
Amy Harrington (court appointed counsel) appeared by zoom for John Cox.

The court noted that it does not have receipts for the gifts. Mr. Siracusa stated that they have been making annual exclusion gifts since 2017, that the court has requested that they file receipts, and that the disbursements always shows up in the accounting. Mr. Siracusa also stated that Mr. Cox does not want any form of change and that he is very irritated with having a conservator. Mr. Siracusa also stated that his client visits with Mr. Cox, that Mr. Cox has refused to have the carpets cleaned or having an attendant, provided history, that his client does not go into the house, that they meet at a local coffee shop and stated that his client will continue to monitor this. Mrs. Harrington stated that Mr. Cox is incredibly independent and a member of the community, and that he has her number and reaches out to her when needed. The court waives any minor defects that may exist. The Fifth Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the Fifth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are under submission with the court's intent to grant.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Aguerre. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-25-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-14-298385 The John C. Cox Trust and PCN-14-298386 Conservatorship of John Cox.

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa appeared by zoom for Bennett Hong (Trustee/Conservator).

Mr. Siracusa stated that Mr. Cox seems to be doing well, that he has his routine, that he purchases his own items himself, that Mr. Cox is very active at his Church (Mission Dolores) and that even with COVID he is able to do much of the things he has been doing. The Court raised the issue of income being spent on Mr. Cox, stated that the trust document says that income should be available to Mr. Cox and that it wants to make the Trustee is cognitive. Mr. Siracusa stated that Mr. Cox has an allowance account, that all of the income and principal is available to Mr. Cox, that it is a living trust, not a third party, and that the beneficiaries are residual beneficiaries. Mr. Siracusa also stated that he is not compelled to address any other issues. The Third Account of Trustee and Petition for Fees, etc. and the Third Account of Conservator and Petition for Fees, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Bennett Hong.

Mr. Siracusa addressed the Court's concerns with respect to the allowance to the Conservatee being greater than what the order allows stating that Mr. Cox's disability changes, that having a routine is very helpful to Mr. Cox, that Mr. Hong was going out to see Mr. Cox every week, that Mr. Hong will now go out twice a month, that Mr. Hong has scaled back on the amount of visits and that he will get a declaration regarding the investments to the Court by the end of the week (11/16). Mr. Hong stated that he has made no changes to the investments. The Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-07-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Continued To Sep-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Bennett Hong. Shaun Carberry for and with John Cox.

Mr. Siracusa reported that Mr. Hong requested the guideline fee of $35,104.00, that he voluntarily reduces it by 25%, that all of the beneficiaries received a copy of the petition, that there have been no objections, that this is not a simple trust, that the conservatee suffers from anxiety disorder and requires time, that the conservatee is very involved, that if this was a bank serving as trustee it would request 1.5% and would not provide the service being provided by Mr. Hong nor the visits to the conservatee. The Court asked for time records. Mr. Siracusa stated that they can pull together time records if necessary, that they are not asking for an hourly rate, that it seems like a different request, that the services are intermingled between the trust and the conservatorship, that they ask that they not be required to proved the time records, that they are asking for the guideline fee and only .75% at that, that it seems to depend on who the probate examiner is as to the request for time records, that it feels like it depends on who reviews the file and that it is not fair to the trustee or conservator. The Court stated that it appreciates the comments regarding the lack of uniformity and asked what would be the difficulty in providing time records. Mr. Siracusa stated that the difficulty is pulling and parsing between what is done for the conservatorship and what is done for the trust, that even a discussion with the financial advisor is for both matters, that the assets have all been transferred into the trust and that the conservatorship accounting was pre-granted. The Court stated that it will consider counsel's concerns and will continue the matter two weeks for the times records to be submitted. Mr. Carberry stated that he is in agreement with Mr. Siracusa, that the guideline fee is fine with either percentage, that the difficulty is that the two matters are going at the same time and that Mr. Hong has done a great job. The accounting petition was continued to September 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the time records to be filed and will be under submission if everything is in order.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Invest Funds (Probate Code 2570)
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, for Petitioner NBennett Hong; Shaun Carberry, for conservatee John Cox.
Mr. Siracusa stated that the because there was no petition for approval of fees before the Court at this time, the Court should not consider any proposed reduction of trustees guideline fees to offset fund manager's fees in ruling upon the petition. He stated that the investment proposal by the Petitioner/Conservator presents a balanced approach to investment of the conservatee's portfolio. The Court noted that the current investment portfolio was in need of adjustment, and inquired if the proposed change in investment would be done right away. Mr. Siracusa stated that the plan was to make a gradual change over time rather than to change the portfolio all at once. Mr. Carberry stated that the conservatee had strong views as to how he wished his money to be invested. His desire was for the portfolio to be divided with 50% in treasury bills and 50% in dividend yield holdings. He wants the investments to be restricted to socially responsible investments, and also to be restricted in accordance with his religious beliefs. Mr. Siracusa stated that it was the conservator's intention to seek input from Mr. Cox in making investment decisions.
The Court expressed a concern with regard to the making of a gift to Patricia for the benefit of Mitra. Mr. Siracusa stated that making the gift in this manner could open up a hornet's nest if, for example, Patricia might pass away without transferring the gift to Mitra, but that this was the way the family has done such transfers, which are made on an annual basis. The opined that it would be more expensive to set up a separate trust for the benefit of Mitra for such a small amount. Mr. Carberry suggested as a viable alternative the proposal that Patricia set up a separate bank account payable on death to Mitra for the funds to be deposited in. Mr. Siracusa was agreeable with this proposal, and agreed to submit to the Court a revised order which provided for handling the gift to Mitra in this manner. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
